title: Oakland's Ryan Coogler wins first Oscar, for 'Sinners' screenplay
text: Oakland filmmaker Ryan Coogler earned his first Academy Award for Original Screenplay for the vampire film "Sinners," which became a historic night at the 2026 Oscars. The film set in 1932 Mississippi Delta garnered four total awards. Michael B. Jordan claimed Best Actor for his dual roles as twin brothers, becoming only the sixth Black male actor to win in this category. Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history as the first woman to win for cinematography, and composer Ludwig Göransson received his second Oscar for Original Score. "Sinners" shattered records with 16 Academy Award nominations, surpassing the previous record of 14. The film also dominated earlier award seasons, earning 13 BAFTA nominations and becoming the most-awarded film by a Black director in BAFTA history. Coogler expressed gratitude during his acceptance, saying: "I grew up in Oakland and Richmond, California and we can talk a lot." The filmmaker has partnered with Jordan since 2013's "Fruitvale Station" and co-founded production company Proximity Media in 2021 with his wife Zinzi and collaborator Sev Ohanian.
